content: Vaccines are like tiny teachers that help your body learn how to fight off germs. Inside your body, you have special defenders called the immune system that protects you from getting sick. When a germ tries to make you sick, your immune system fights it by recognizing it and attacking it. Vaccines give your immune system a tiny, harmless piece of a germ so it can practice fighting without getting you sick. After practicing, your immune system remembers the germ and can fight it quickly if it ever shows up for real. This means that vaccines help keep you safe by stopping germs before they can make you sick. Vaccines are tested carefully to make sure they are safe, and millions of people get them to stay healthy. You can get vaccines as a little shot or sometimes as drops, and they protect you from many different diseases. When lots of people get vaccines, it helps keep everyone safe by stopping germs from spreading. So, vaccines are like superhero training for your body, helping it stay strong and healthy every day!
